source_quote: During the period from the middle of August through the end of October, the Company developed the Profit Improvement Plan and prepared for a sale process. During this period, J.P. Morgan was contacted by 27 potential participants in a sale process, including three strategic parties (not counting Industry Participant) and 24 financial participants (including potential lead buyers as well as large suppliers of equity capital to lead buyers).

Filing narrates two unnamed strategic parties that were contacted by Barclays but did not sign NDAs; one responded 6/3/2016 that it was not interested, another responded 6/11/2016. §M1 skip (no NDA, no price, no bid intent). Source p.32.

Sponsor B: range $17.00-$18.00/share; §G1 structural signal ⇒ informal; §P-G2 range-satisfier path (no bid_type_inference_note required).

TB IOI accompanied by equity commitment letter and draft merger agreement (both §G1 formal triggers) but filing explicitly labels it 'non-binding' — informal label takes precedence per §G1 but flagging for adjudication.

Filing describes the $19.25 offer as 'revised written, non-binding proposal' yet also explicitly 'best and final offer' — the 'best and final' trigger wins per §G1 precedence; 'non-binding' label reflects legal status pre-execution. Formal classification upheld.

27 potential participants contacted JPM (3 strategics ex-IP + 24 financials); only 15 signed NDAs. The 12 other contacts gave no price or bid intent per §M1 and are not emitted.
source_quote: During the period from the middle of August through the end of October, the Company developed the Profit Improvement Plan and prepared for a sale process. During this period, J.P. Morgan was contacted by 27 potential participants in a sale process, including three strategic parties (not counting Industry Participant) and 24 financial participants (including potential lead buyers as well as large suppliers of equity capital to lead buyers).
